You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>

- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A scrapbook layout displays pastel watercolor washes and die-cut stickers like "lovely," "happy," and a flamingo, surrounded by patterned paper and ceramic dishes, while white text overlay reads "DIE CUT & WATERCOLOR PAINTING TECHNIQUE." [4-8] The camera zooms in to show close-up details including a gold butterfly, a neon green butterfly, and translucent tags reading "that's so fancy," "just chillin'," and "the sweet" atop the colorful washes. [8-12] The perspective shifts to the bottom left, revealing a yellow pineapple sticker, a silver ampersand, gold script reading "pretty," and a girl in a pink dress, with "Hello there" patterned paper visible beneath the kraft page.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The top strip of the scrapbook layout featuring a flamingo and banner remains visible at the top edge. Below this, the colorful watercolor background is replaced by a large expanse of plain brown kraft paper, indicating the decorated sheet is being lifted up.

[14-16] A hand holds the bottom right corner of a large, plain sheet of light brown kraft paper, bringing it into full view. The solid tan surface dominates the frame, replacing the colorful watercolor background and die-cuts with a blank, textured page against a white backdrop.

[16-18] A hand holding a white bottle with a nozzle positions over the center of the brown kraft paper. The hand squeezes the container, dispensing a thick, white substance in a zig-zag pattern down the middle of the page, while a second hand stabilizes the bottom left corner.

[18-20] A hand holding a paintbrush enters the frame and begins spreading the white zig-zag line of substance across the brown paper. The brush smears the white paint back and forth near the top, while another hand stabilizes the left edge of the sheet.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of actions taken to prepare the new paper base starting from the page lift.

answers.text: The provided video evidence does not contain the requested sequence of actions. The clips from 0 to 10 seconds only show static views and camera zooms of a completed scrapbook page with watercolor washes and die-cut stickers. There is no footage of a hand lifting a page, holding a tan sheet, applying glue in a zigzag motion, or spreading paint with a brush as described in the target answer.

samples.output: <think>The paintbrush smears the white substance into a wide, horizontal band near the top of the brown paper. As the brush moves down, it blends the remaining zig-zag lines, creating a larger, opaque white section against the tan background while a hand holds the paper steady.</think><answer></answer>

The clips from 0 to 10 seconds only show static views and camera zooms of a completed scrapbook page with watercolor washes and die-cut stickers. There is no footage of a hand lifting a page, holding a tan sheet, applying glue in a zigzag motion, or spreading paint with a brush as described in the target answer.

The provided video evidence concludes at the 12-second mark, showing only a static view of the scrapbook's bottom-left corner with various stickers and patterned paper. There is no visual information available beyond this point to describe the subsequent actions. Consequently, the sequence involving lifting the page, holding a tan sheet, applying glue in a zigzag motion, or spreading paint with a brush is not present in the footage. The requested preparation steps cannot be described based on the available clips.

The available evidence only shows the decorated page being lifted at 12 seconds, revealing plain brown kraft paper underneath by 14 seconds. The footage ends here, so subsequent actions like holding a tan sheet vertically, applying glue in a zigzag pattern, or spreading paint with a brush are not visible. Therefore, the full preparation sequence described in the target answer cannot be confirmed or detailed using the provided video clips.

The visible sequence begins at 12 seconds as the decorated page lifts, revealing plain brown kraft paper underneath. By 14 seconds, a hand holds this large tan sheet fully in view against a white backdrop, replacing the previous watercolor design. The provided footage concludes here, showing only the exposure and positioning of the new base material. Actions such as applying glue or spreading paint mentioned in the target answer are not present in the available video segments.

Building on the revealed kraft paper, the sequence continues at 16 seconds as a hand stabilizes the sheet's corner while another dispenses thick white glue from a nozzle bottle. This substance is applied in a distinct zig-zag pattern down the center of the brown surface. Although the target answer mentions paint spreading at 18 seconds, the provided evidence strictly concludes with the glue application step, showing the adhesive being laid onto the new base material.

samples.output: <think>A hand wearing a floral sleeve moves a green paintbrush to a pink paint pan within the watercolor palette on the left and dips it in. The pink rose die-cut sheet remains stationary on the white painted background to the right as the brush loads with color.</think><answer></answer>

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A scrapbook layout displays pastel watercolor washes and die-cut stickers like "lovely," "happy," and a flamingo, surrounded by patterned paper and ceramic dishes, while white text overlay reads "DIE CUT & WATERCOLOR PAINTING TECHNIQUE." [4-8] The camera zooms in to show close-up details including a gold butterfly, a neon green butterfly, and translucent tags reading "that's so fancy," "just chillin'," and "the sweet" atop the colorful washes. [8-12] The perspective shifts to the bottom left, revealing a yellow pineapple sticker, a silver ampersand, gold script reading "pretty," and a girl in a pink dress, with "Hello there" patterned paper visible beneath the kraft page.

[14-16] A hand holds a large sheet of light brown kraft paper against a white backdrop. [16-18] A hand squeezes a white bottle, dispensing a thick white substance in a zig-zag pattern down the center while another hand stabilizes the corner. [18-28] A paintbrush spreads the white substance across the brown paper. The brush smears the zig-zag lines back and forth, starting at the top and working downwards. This blends the substance into a large, opaque, textured block of white paint covering the center. A left hand wearing a silver ring holds the paper steady on the white surface throughout the process.

From 28s to 40s, a right hand uses a green-handled paintbrush to spread thick white paint over the center of a brown sheet. The brush maneuvers vertically, moving up and down to blend the paint into a large, opaque, textured block. The white area expands slightly as the bristles smooth the substance, though horizontal stroke marks remain visible. Meanwhile, a left hand wearing a silver ring on the ring finger rests on the left edge of the paper, stabilizing it against the underlying white surface throughout the painting process.

[40-46] A right hand maneuvers a green-handled paintbrush downwards and vertically, blending thick white paint into a large, opaque block on brown paper. The strokes smooth the substance, expanding the white coverage slightly while maintaining a textured surface, particularly extending the block downwards near the bottom center. A left hand wearing a silver ring stabilizes the paper's left edge against the white background. [46-48] The painting hands disappear, and a right hand enters from the right edge. [48-52] The right hand holds a white hairdryer over the wet white paint, moving it back and forth from the right edge towards the center to dry the surface. A finger stabilizes the bottom right corner of the paper against the white surface.

[52-54] A right hand maneuvers a white device with a visible black cord over the white painted area. [54-58] A digital webpage displays "DEAR LIZZY - ROSE FILIGREE PAPER" by American Crafts, featuring a pink rose outline design, "REGULAR CUT" label, $0.99 price, and teal "add to cart" button. [58-64] The view returns to a craft table with two watercolor palettes on the left. A hand wearing a dark floral sleeve positions a pink die-cut sheet with rose outlines onto the white painted background, then uses a green-handled paintbrush to dip into the colorful paints.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The top strip of the scrapbook layout featuring a flamingo and banner remains visible at the top edge. Below this, the colorful watercolor background is replaced by a large expanse of plain brown kraft paper, indicating the decorated sheet is being lifted up.

[64-66] A hand wearing a floral sleeve moves a green paintbrush to the watercolor palette on the left. The brush dips into a yellow paint pan to load color. The pink rose die-cut remains stationary on the white painted background to the right throughout this action.

[66-68] A person's arm, clad in a floral sleeve, reaches toward the watercolor palette on the left. The green-handled brush dips into a yellow paint pan. The pink rose die-cut sheet remains stationary on the white painted background to the right.

[68-70] A hand wearing a floral sleeve moves a green paintbrush to a pink paint pan within the watercolor palette on the left and dips it in. The pink rose die-cut sheet remains stationary on the white painted background to the right as the brush loads with color.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A scrapbook layout displays pastel watercolor washes and die-cut stickers like "lovely," "happy," and a flamingo, surrounded by patterned paper and ceramic dishes, while white text overlay reads "DIE CUT & WATERCOLOR PAINTING TECHNIQUE." [4-8] The camera zooms in to show close-up details including a gold butterfly, a neon green butterfly, and translucent tags reading "that's so fancy," "just chillin'," and "the sweet" atop the colorful washes. [8-12] The perspective shifts to the bottom left, revealing a yellow pineapple sticker, a silver ampersand, gold script reading "pretty," and a girl in a pink dress, with "Hello there" patterned paper visible beneath the kraft page. [14-16] A hand holds a large sheet of light brown kraft paper against a white backdrop. [16-18] A hand squeezes a white bottle, dispensing a thick white substance in a zig-zag pattern down the center while another hand stabilizes the corner. [18-28] A paintbrush spreads the white substance across the brown paper. The brush smears the zig-zag lines back and forth, starting at the top and working downwards. This blends the substance into a large, opaque, textured block of white paint covering the center. A left hand wearing a silver ring holds the paper steady

Between 78s and 90s, a hand wearing a floral sleeve repeatedly dips a green paintbrush into a yellow paint pan within a watercolor palette on the left. The hand moves the brush to the right, applying the yellow pigment onto the white background visible through the upper-left section of a pink rose die-cut stencil. This cycle of reloading the brush with yellow paint and dabbing it onto the top-left rose design occurs multiple times, gradually adding color to the artwork while the stencil remains stationary on the paper.

[90-92] A hand wearing a floral sleeve moves a green paintbrush from the top-left rose area to the watercolor palette on the left to reload with yellow pigment. [92-102] The hand then dabs the yellow paint onto the top-left rose design visible through the pink die-cut stencil, maneuvering the brush slightly lower over time. Meanwhile, a second hand wearing a silver ring stabilizes the paper near the palette, which holds rows of vibrant circular paint pans, keeping the pink stencil stationary on the white background.

[102-112] A hand wearing a floral sleeve uses a green paintbrush to apply yellow watercolor through a pink rose stencil onto a white background. Initially, the brush dabs pigment onto the top-left rose area while a second hand with a silver ring stabilizes the paper near a vibrant watercolor palette. The artist then moves the brush down to paint a lower rose cutout, followed by shifting from a bottom-left rose to a bottom-right one, continuing to dab yellow pigment. Finally, the hand lifts the brush from the bottom-right section and returns to the palette on the left to reload yellow paint from a circular pan, while the stabilizing hand keeps the stencil fixed in place.

[112-124] A hand wearing a floral sleeve moves a green paintbrush from the artwork to the watercolor palette on the left, dipping it into a yellow pan in the bottom row to reload pigment. A second hand with a silver ring rests near the palette edge. The brush then moves rightward toward the pink rose stencil fixed on the paper. The artist positions the brush over the lower-left rose cutout and dabs the yellow watercolor onto the white background visible through the stencil's petals. The second hand stabilizes the paper near the bottom edge as the yellow pigment spreads across the design.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The top strip of the scrapbook layout featuring a flamingo and banner remains visible at the top edge. Below this, the colorful watercolor background is replaced by a large expanse of plain brown kraft paper, indicating the decorated sheet is being lifted up.

[124-126] A hand wearing a floral sleeve uses a green paintbrush to dab yellow watercolor onto the lower-left rose cutout of the pink stencil. After applying pigment to the white background visible through the petals, the hand moves the brush back toward the palette on the left to reload paint.

[140-142] The pink die-cut stencil is completely lifted away from the brown kraft paper, fully revealing the underlying watercolor artwork. Soft, blended roses in shades of yellow, pink, blue, and green are visible on the surface. The white paint palette remains stationary on the left side of the frame.
